20世纪50年代，芝加哥的一个黑人家庭正在努力找回失去的祖先遗产，同时在HBO的“洛夫克拉夫村”中抵御怪物和魔法。这部电影改编自马特·拉夫(Matt Ruff)2016年的同名黑暗奇幻/恐怖小说“爱的世界”(The Lovcraft Country)。与激发灵感的小说一样，这部电视剧将超自然的洛夫克拉夫特式的恐怖与那个时代更平凡但同样可怕的种族不平等进行了尖锐的并列，尤其是在这一年里，针对警察残忍杀害黑人男性(和女性)的民权抗议活动广泛发生，这一年尤其及时。撇开社会相关性不谈，它也是纯粹的娱乐。
背景设定在20世纪50年代的吉姆·克罗时代，拉夫的这本书的结构是一系列短篇小说，尽管每件事都是相互关联的。这本书的第一节集中在阿提库斯身上，他是一名参加过朝鲜战争的黑人老兵，也是惠普·洛夫克拉夫特的铁杆粉丝，尽管作者有臭名昭著的种族主义。当他疏远的父亲在遇到一名衣着光鲜的白人男子驾驶一辆银色凯迪拉克后失踪时，他留下了一条神秘的信息，阿提库斯开始了从芝加哥南区到马萨诸塞州乡村的公路旅行。陪同他的还有他的叔叔乔治(“安全黑人旅游指南”的出版商)和他儿时的朋友利蒂夏(又名莱蒂)。
随后的故事集中在利蒂夏、乔治的妻子希波利塔和他们的女儿等人身上，让大家庭中的每一位成员都有了在讲故事的聚光灯下的时间。对于精明的读者来说，一路上有很多关于洛夫克拉夫特作品的狡猾引用：一个名为古代黎明秩序的秘密阴谋，一座闹鬼的房子，一个奇怪的袖珍宇宙，时移，变形人，还有一本被诅咒的书。但最可怕的怪物不是妖魔恐怖，也不是林中的幕府将军，而是我们的主人公在旅途中遇到的赤裸裸的种族主义和偏执。
制片人米莎·格林(“地下城”、“无政府之子”)在这部改编自HBO的影片中保留了这本书的大部分核心前提和主要人物。就像在书中一样，阿提克斯(乔纳森·梅格斯饰，旧金山最后的黑人)在朝鲜战争期间在海外呆了一段时间后返回芝加哥，发现他的父亲蒙特罗斯(迈克尔·K·威廉姆斯饰)已经消失了。他招募了他的叔叔乔治·B·万斯(Courtney B.Vance，The People v.O.J.Simpson)和他一起去马萨诸塞州的阿德姆镇(Ardham)和莱蒂(Jurnee Smollett，猛禽)一起兜风。这并不是一条平坦的旅程，种族主义的当地人把他们赶出了小镇，枪声熊熊，树林里有恶毒的怪物，但他们最终到达了阿德姆·洛奇，布雷斯韦特家族的祖籍--这才是徒步旅行真正开始的地方。
这就是说，有几个人离开了。有些是相对较小的调整，比如将布雷斯韦特继承人凯勒(Caleb)换成一个女人克里斯蒂娜(Christina，艾比·李(Abbey Lee)，《疯狂的麦克斯：狂暴之路》(Mad Max：Fury Road))，以及将古代黎明的顺序改为亚当之子(Sons Of Adam)。由于克里斯蒂娜是女儿，而不是儿子，她被排除在修道会中占据她应有的位置。自然，她对此相当不满，这使她成为阿提库斯、乔治和莱蒂不太可能(尽管不值得信任)的盟友，至少只要他们能为她别有用心的目的服务。在该系列中，凯勒布的角色变成了克里斯蒂娜英俊的金发追随者威廉(乔丹·帕特里克·史密斯饰)。
这本书的其他偏离要戏剧性得多，最著名的是一集时空旅行，在这一集中，莱蒂、阿提库斯和蒙特罗斯试图找回神秘的名字之书的副本，以及大部分大结局和某些关键人物的(推测)命运。该系列还加入了一个全新的故事，灵感来自韩国神话：阿提库斯在韩国服兵役，以及他与一位名叫Ji-ah(杰米·钟，才华横溢)的年轻护士的短暂恋情，而Ji-ah被九尾狐的精灵附身(Kumiho)(九尾狐狸的精灵)。(注：阿提库斯·阿提克斯曾在韩国服兵役，他与一位年轻护士Ji-Ah(杰米·钟，杰米·钟饰)有过短暂的恋情，后者被九尾狐狸的灵魂附身。)。我不想破坏这件事对你的影响，我只想说，与久美浩发生性关系对她的伴侣来说通常都是糟糕的结局。
这是极大的格林的信用作为一个作家和制片人，这些变化中的大多数加强了适应，同时保留了拉夫的原始愿景的优势。她显然对类型很感兴趣，在一集中唤起了印第安纳·琼斯式的冒险，在另一集中唤起了经典的鬼屋故事，在下一集中唤起了涉及多元宇宙的科幻故事。然而，这场演出给人的感觉仍然是一个无缝的整体。格林甚至找到了一种聪明的、非常元的方式，将小说“洛夫克拉夫特乡村”融入到该系列更广泛的叙事中。
拉夫的小说巧妙地颠覆了这部小说，因为它在一位以极端种族主义观点闻名的小说家的超自然恐怖中反映了种族主义在美国的恐怖-事实上，洛夫克拉夫特的文学遗产正在被科幻圈子里的许多人根据这些观点重新评估。在试播集中，阿提库斯
黑人历史和文化元素构成了故事的基本背景。例如，乔治的旅行指南显然是受到黑人驾车者绿皮书(这本书反过来启发了2018年奥斯卡获奖电影绿皮书)的启发，而所谓的日落小镇在20世纪50年代确实对黑人旅行者构成了非常真实的危险。在该系列中，格林巧妙地融入了额外的关键元素，以扩展这些试金石。例如，从著名小说家和剧作家詹姆斯·鲍德温(James Baldwin)1965年与小威廉·F·巴克利(William F.Buckley，Jr.)的辩论中，有一段画外音摘录贯穿了整个试播过程。关于种族隔离和美国梦。(鲍德温雄辩地辩称，美国梦是以美国黑人为代价的。)。
然后是修长、浅色皮肤的莱蒂和她同父异母的妹妹露比(温米·莫萨库饰，路德饰)之间可以理解的紧张关系。难怪露比发现自己受到威廉提供的药水的诱惑，这种药水可以让她暂时变成一个白人女人(杰米·诺伊曼和杰西卡·琼斯饰)--无论恢复成红宝石的过程有多可怕。
在另一集中，乔治和希波利塔的小女儿戴安娜(居民贾达·哈里斯Jada Harris饰)(也是书中的性别转换)哀悼了14岁的埃米特·蒂尔(Emmett Till)被残忍地私刑处死-她是20世纪50年代民权运动的标志性人物。当戴安娜被当地一名白人警察队长(麦克·勃兰特饰)诅咒时，她受到了汤姆叔叔小屋中一个名叫托普西的角色的启发，被一对双胞胎恶魔女孩追赶。1921年塔尔萨种族大屠杀的阴影笼罩着我们所有的主要人物，他们的许多祖先在暴力中丧生。
有一些来自主要演员阵容的精彩表演，特别是Mosaku，Williams，Majors和Smollet，特别向饰演戴安娜的贾达·哈里斯喊话，当她意识到诅咒的本质时，她真的让你感觉到她的角色的恐惧和脆弱-然后她顽固而坚定的决心要去荡秋千。如果要我对这部连续剧吹毛求疵的话，那就是尽管有一些令人眼花缭乱的魔法烟火，但结局最终没有多少意义。幸运的是，到那个时候，我对角色和他们引人入胜的故事投入了足够的精力，所以我很愿意在这一点上让这部剧松一口气。
老实说，我不知道“洛夫克拉夫特乡村”第二季会去哪里讲故事--甚至不知道会不会有第二季，尽管大结局在收视率上大获全胜，这对该剧的未来来说是个好兆头。格林最近在大结局后的采访中告诉Deadline好莱坞，我设想第二季将延续马特·拉夫小说的精神，继续收回有色人种通常被排除在外的讲故事的流派空间，这一季将继续延续马特·拉夫小说的精神。在大结局结束后的一次采访中，格林告诉Deadline好莱坞，这一季继承了马特·拉夫小说的精神。基于第一季的整体出色表现，我相信她会想出一些有创意、娱乐性和发人深省的东西。在这里，我希望她能得到这个机会。
note_en:
A Black family in 1950s Chicago struggles to reclaim their lost ancestral legacy while warding off monsters and magic spells in HBO's   Lovecraft Country, based on the 2016 dark fantasy/horror novel  of the same name by Matt Ruff. Like the novel that inspired it, the series' pointed juxtaposition of supernatural Lovecraftian horrors against more mundane, but equally horrifying racial inequalities of that era is especially timely in a year that has seen widespread civil rights protests against the brutal killings of Black men (and women) by police officers. And social relevance aside, it also works as pure entertainment.
  Set in the Jim Crow era of the 1950s, Ruff's  book is structured as a series of short stories, although everything is inter-related. The first quarter of the book focuses on Atticus, a black Korean war veteran and big  H.P. Lovecraft fan, despite the author's notorious racism. When his estranged father disappears after encountering a well-dressed white man driving a silver Cadillac, leaving a cryptic message, Atticus sets out on a road trip from Chicago's South Side to rural Massachusetts. He's accompanied by his Uncle George—publisher of  The Safe Negro Travel Guide—and his childhood friend Letitia (aka Leti).
 Subsequent stories focus on Letitia, George's wife, Hippolyta, and their daughter, among others, giving each member of the extended family their time in the storytelling spotlight. There are plenty of sly references to the  works of Lovecraft for the astute reader along the way: a secret cabal called the Order of the Ancient Dawn, a haunted house, a strange pocket universe, time shifting, shape-shifters, and a cursed book. But the worst monsters are not eldritch terrors or a  Shoggoth in the woods; it's the stark racism and bigotry our protagonists encounter along the way.
 Showrunner Misha Green ( Underground, Sons of Anarchy) preserves much of the book's central premise and main characters in this HBO adaptation. As in the book, Atticus (Jonathan Majors,  The Last Black Man in San Francisco) returns to Chicago after a stint overseas during the Korean War and finds his father, Montrose (Michael K. Williams,  The Wire) has vanished. He recruits his uncle, George  (Courtney B. Vance,  The People v. O.J. Simpson), to join him on a road trip to the town of Ardham, Massachusetts, and Leti (Jurnee Smollett,  Birds of Prey) tags along for the ride. It's not a smooth journey, what with the racist locals running them out of town, guns blazing, and vicious monsters in the woods, but they eventually arrive at Ardham Lodge, ancestral home to the Braithwaite clan—which is when the hijinks truly begin.
 That said, there are several departures. Some are relatively minor tweaks, like gender-swapping the Braithwaite heir, Caleb, into a woman, Christina (Abbey Lee,  Mad Max: Fury Road), and changing the Order of the Ancient Dawn to the Sons of Adam. Since Christina is a daughter, not a son, she is excluded from taking her rightful place in the order. Naturally she's pretty bitter about that, making her an unlikely (albeit untrustworthy) ally of sorts for Atticus, George, and Leti, at least as long as they can serve her ulterior purpose. The character of Caleb morphs into Christina's handsome blond henchman, William (Jordan Patrick Smith,  Vikings), in the series.
    Other departures from the book are much more dramatic, most notably a time-travel episode where Leti, Atticus, and Montrose try to recover a copy of the mystical Book of Names, as well as much of the finale and the (presumed) fates of certain key characters. There's also an entirely new story written into the series, inspired by  Korean mythology: a flashback to Atticus' military service in South Korea and his brief romance with a young nurse named Ji-Ah (Jamie Chung,  The Gifted), who is possessed by a   Kumiho (a "Nine-Tailed Fox" spirit). I'm not going to spoil the implications of that for you, except to say that sex with a  Kumiho typically ends badly for her partners.
 It is greatly to Green's credit as a writer and producer that most of these changes enhance the adaptation while retaining the strengths of Ruff's original vision. She's clearly having fun with genre, evoking an Indiana Jones-style adventure in one episode, a classic haunted house story in another, and a science fiction story involving the multiverse in the next. Yet the show still feels like a seamless whole. Green even finds a clever, very "meta" way to work the novel,  Lovecraft Country, into the broader narrative of the series.
  Ruff's novel is ingeniously subversive in how it reflects the horrors of racism in the United States in the supernatural horrors conjured by a novelist famous for his  extreme racist views—indeed, Lovecraft's literary legacy is  being re-evaluated by many in the science fiction community in light of those views. In the pilot episode, Atticus recalls how his father made him memorize an  infamously racist Lovecraft poem as a child to counter the boy's love of Lovecraftian horror.
 Elements of Black history and culture form an essential backdrop to the tales. For instance, George's travel guide is obviously inspired by   The Negro Motorist Green Book (which in turn inspired the 2018 Oscar-winning film,   Green Book), and  so-called " sundown towns" really did pose a very real danger to Black travelers in the 1950s. For the series, Green skillfully weaves in additional key elements to expand on those touchstones. For example, there's a voiceover excerpt running throughout the pilot from famed novelist and playwright James Baldwin's  1965 debate against William F. Buckley, Jr. on segregation and the American dream. (Baldwin eloquently argued that the "American dream is at the expense of the American Negro.")
 Then there's the understandable tension between the slender, light-skinned Leti and her darker half-sister Ruby (Wunmi Mosaku,  Luther). Small wonder Ruby finds herself tempted by William's offer of a potion that can temporarily turn her into a white woman (Jamie Neumann,  Jessica Jones)—no matter how gruesome the process of returning to her Ruby form might be.
  In another episode, Diana (Jada Harris,  The Resident), George and Hippolyta's young daughter (also gender-swapped from the book), mourns the brutal lynching of 14-year-old  Emmett Till—an iconic figure of the Civil Rights movement in the 1950s. When Diana is cursed by a local white police captain (Mac Brandt), she is pursued by twin demon girls,  inspired by a character named Topsy from   Uncle Tom's Cabin. And the shadow of the 1921  Tulsa race massacre hangs over all our main characters, many of whose ancestors perished in the violence.
 There are some truly fine performances from the main cast, especially Mosaku, Williams, Majors, and Smollet, with a special shoutout to Jada Harris as Diana, who really makes you feel her character's terror and vulnerability when she realizes the nature of the curse—and then her stubborn, steely resolve to go down swinging. If I have one quibble with the series, it's that the finale ultimately doesn't make a whole lot of sense, despite some dazzling magical pyrotechnics. Fortunately, by that time, I was sufficiently invested in the characters and their compelling stories that I was quite willing to cut the show some slack on that score.
 I honestly don't know where  Lovecraft Country would go for a second season of storytelling—or even if there will be a second season, although the fact that the finale posted  a big ratings win bodes well for the show's future. "I envision a second season that carries on the spirit of Matt Ruff’s novel by continuing to reclaim the genre storytelling space that people of color have typically been left out of," Green  recently told Deadline Hollywood in a (spoiler-filled) post-finale interview. Based on the overall excellence of this first season, I trust her to come up with something inventive, entertaining, and thought-provoking. Here's hoping she gets that chance.
title: “洛夫克拉夫特乡村”巧妙地颠覆了臭名昭著的种族主义作家。
title_en: Lovecraft Country is a cleverly subversive take on notoriously racist author

note:
夏天!。这是一段美好的时光，但也是一段炙手可热的时光！事实上，大多数人会争辩说，如果没有好的空调，你就不能度过夏天。但如果你的建筑物或汽车从未加热怎么办？
普渡大学的工程师已经开发出一种白色涂料，可以让所有表面保持凉爽--具体地说，比周围环境低18华氏度(-7.778摄氏度)。此外，这种超白涂料能反射95.5%的阳光，并有效地辐射红外线热量。
普渡大学机械工程教授阮秀林(音译)表示：“阳光直射下的表面温度比当地气象站报告的那个地区的温度要低，这是非常违反直觉的，但我们已经证明这是可能的，”普渡大学机械工程教授阮秀林(音译)说。
这种涂料的工作原理是几乎不吸收太阳能，并将热量从使用的地方散发出去。它甚至可以通过将热量从地球送到深空，在那里它不再影响我们的星球，从而帮助应对气候变化。
我们不会将热量从地表转移到大气中。我们只是把它们全部倾倒到宇宙中，宇宙是一个无限的散热器，麻省理工学院博士后研究员李翔宇(音译)说，他在阮的实验室读博士后时参与了这个项目。
为了证明他们的新产品的有效性，普渡大学的研究人员在屋顶实验中拍摄了他们的油漆与普通白色油漆的红外摄像机图像。结果简直令人印象深刻。
“红外线相机可以给你一个温度读数，就像温度计判断某人是否发烧一样。这些读数证实，我们的油漆比周围环境和商业同类产品的温度都要低。“阮说。
实验证明，普渡的新型涂料可以在阳光直射下保持较低的温度，并能反射更多的紫外线。现在，想象一下这种油漆在我们城市的所有建筑和道路上都使用过。这难道不是对抗全球变暖的创造性方式吗？
note_en:
Summer! It's a wonderful time but a hot one! Indeed, most would argue that you cannot survive summer without some good air conditioning. But what if your buildings or cars never heated up?
 Purdue University engineers  have developed a white paint that can keep all surfaces cool — specifically, up to  18 degrees Fahrenheit (-7.778°C) cooler than their ambient surroundings. Moreover, the "super-white" paint reflects  95.5% sunlight and efficiently radiates infrared heat.
   "It’s very counterintuitive for a surface in direct sunlight to be cooler than the temperature your local weather station reports for that area, but we’ve shown this to be possible,"  said Xiulin Ruan, a Purdue professor of mechanical engineering.
 The paint works by absorbing nearly no solar energy and sending heat away from wherever it is used. It can even help with  climate change by sending heat away from Earth into deep space where it can no longer affect our planet.
  "We’re not moving heat from the surface to the atmosphere. We’re just dumping it all out into the universe, which is an infinite heat sink,"  said Xiangyu Li, a postdoctoral researcher at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology who worked on this project as a Ph.D. student in Ruan’s lab.
  To prove the efficacy of their new product, the Purdue researchers took infrared camera images of their paint compared to regular white paint in rooftop experiments. The results were nothing short of impressive.
  “An infrared camera gives you a temperature reading just like a thermometer would to judge if someone has a fever. These readings confirmed that our paint has a lower temperature than both its surroundings and the commercial counterpart,” Ruan said.
    The experiment proved that Purdue's novel paint can maintain a lower temperature under direct sunlight and reflect more  ultraviolet rays. Now, imagine this paint used all over our cities on all buildings and roads. Wouldn't that be a creative way to fight  global warming?
title: 这种新型超白涂料可以给建筑物和汽车降温
title_en: This New Super-White Paint Can Cool Down Buildings and Cars

note:
PrimaryBid允许散户投资者(即普通投资者和非专业投资者)投资于上市公司发行的新股，该公司已经筹集了5,000万美元的新资金。自2020年4月以来，这家初创公司刚刚与较大的投资银行合作，为英国上市公司和信托基金进行了41项融资努力，让散户投资者参与其中。
PrimaryBid首席执行官阿南德·桑巴西万在一份声明中表示：“新冠肺炎疫情证明了公开市场的有效性，企业迅速有效地进行了资本重组。”“我们的技术让数以千计的散户投资者与机构投资者平等参与，为公司发行人打开了一个庞大而重要的流动性和长期股权来源。董事会和他们的顾问对我们的解决方案的反应非常好：他们认识到我们的零售包容数字解决方案将良好的管理和最佳的执行结合在一起。“。
PrimaryBid计划利用这笔资金招聘更多人才，投资于其技术平台，建立更多合作伙伴关系，并在国际上扩张。
与其平台上的投资者不同，B系列来自一系列大牌战略参与者和风投。它们包括伦敦证券交易所集团(London Stock Exchange Group)、Draper ESPRIT、OMERS Ventures、富达国际战略风险投资公司(Fidelity International Strategic Ventures)和荷兰银行风险投资公司(ABN AMRO Ventures)，以及之前的支持者Pentech和Outside Ventures。
伦敦证交所集团可能会为PrimaryBid未来的目标提供一个线索：意大利的博尔萨意大利交易所(Borsa Italiana Exchange)以及绿松石泛欧股票市场也是该集团足迹的一部分。
伦敦证券交易所(London Stock Exchange Plc)一级市场股票和固定收益主管查理·沃克(Charlie Walker)在一份声明中表示：“这笔投资建立在我们与PrimaryBid合作的基础上，是伦敦证券交易所集团(London Stock Exchange Group)扩大散户进入公开股票市场的承诺的一部分。”通过PrimaryBid的创新产品，散户投资者能够以与机构投资者相同的条件获得融资，通过提供额外的资本和流动性来支持英国的上市公司。PrimaryBid已经成为英国融资生态系统的重要组成部分，我们期待着与他们合作，进一步加强散户投资者进入英国国内和全球资本市场的机会。“。
在2019年9月的首轮860万美元的首轮融资之后，这家初创公司没有披露本轮融资的估值。自今年夏天以来，这轮最新的B轮融资一直是谣言的主题(最近一次是昨晚有报道称，这轮融资终于结束了)，
PrimaryBid的增长正值一些初创公司一直在建立针对利基机会的投资服务，以及为那些服务不足的人提供服务的时候。上个月的反弹表明，提供一种投资(而不是购买)收藏品的方式肯定是有钱和机会的；亿利街已经建立了一个平台，向投资者介绍也像航运这样的新投资类别；Stash、Revolut和Robinhood等公司也在将交易和投资带给一个新的消费者阶层。
这并不意味着专注于小投资者和投资市场利基机会的新进入者没有自己的挑战。Revolut面临着围绕高管行为的争议(然而，这些争议似乎已经解决：它仍在筹集数亿美元)。亿利街最近起诉(并胜诉)了一家船舶回收公司，但与此同时，该公司似乎正因其一些行为而受到调查。罗宾汉在今年早些时候搁置了扩张计划后，无限期推迟了在英国推出的计划。
PrimaryBid最近的增长得益于公开市场和投资界动荡的一年。
就像新冠肺炎扰乱了我们生活的其他方面一样，大流行的头几个月见证了世界贸易的大冻结。由于许多人不确定未来几个月在当地和全球经济方面的表现，IPO市场几乎枯竭，交易放缓。
然后，事情开始解冻，在新的条件下，活动逐渐而有效地回升：就目前而言，一切都很遥远。更重要的是，新的竞技场对新球员来说意味着新的机会。
这就是PrimaryBid一直在介入的地方。这家初创公司已经建立了一个平台，让散户投资者更容易参与新股发行，自2016年以来一直存在，但它在筹集资金的公司可能希望把网撒得比往常更大的时候找到了自己的最佳状态。
这家初创公司在4月份领导了一场大型活动，强调散户投资者在帮助股市恢复活跃水平方面可以发挥的作用。自大流行开始以来，提供新股发行渠道的公司包括指南针集团(Compass Group)、Ocado、泰勒·温佩比和塞格罗。
散户投资者本质上是一种长尾游戏。虽然他们个人的投资将比高净值个人或机构投资者少得多，但总的来说，他们的活动占了相当大的比例。根据PrimaryBid的数据，英国国家统计局(Office Of National Statistics)2018年的最新数据估计，散户投资者约占英国股本的13.5%，尽管在富时250指数(FTSE250)成份股公司中，这一比例接近20%，在一些AIM公司，这一比例可能高达30%甚至更高。
还有许多其他平台供普通人购买和交易股票，但PrimaryBid的不同之处在于，它专注于新股发行，而不是出售和交易现有股票。理论上，一家公司还可以为IPO通过PrimaryBid配售股票，但正如一位发言人所描述的那样，“真正的创新是让散户参与‘加速’的后续加薪(按股票发行规模计算，这大约是IPO规模的5倍)，而且从来没有对散户开放(而一些IPO在历史上是这样的)。”
Draper Espirity合伙人维诺斯·贾亚库马尔(Vinoth Jayakumar)表示：“我们对PrimaryBid的投资符合我们更广泛的投资理念的一部分，即让散户投资者进入公开市场的民主化，以及市场基础设施软件的现代化。对我们来说，我们两家公司都在预测金融未来的发展方向。“。
OMERS Ventures是这家多产的加拿大养老基金的投资部门，该公司表示，它是专注于金融科技的集团的一部分。OMERS Ventures合伙人塔拉·里夫斯(Tara Reeves)在一份声明中表示：“作为金融科技的专家，我们不可能忽视PrimaryBid在2020年的崛起及其在资本市场上倡导散户投资者的成功。”PrimaryBid的技术处在金融服务的强大趋势--监管、数字化和民主化--的交汇点上，OMERS风险投资公司很高兴支持该团队让个人投资者与机构处于平等地位的使命。PrimaryBid现在已经很好地融入了英国融资生态系统的最高级别，我们期待着帮助这个团队实现他们在国际上的雄心壮志。“。
富达国际战略风险投资公司(Fidelity International Strategic Ventures)副总裁迈克尔·西姆(Michael Sim)在一份声明中表示：“我们很高兴能与PrimaryBid合作，提高资本市场的公平性、包容性和透明度。”阿南德和团队建立了独特的技术基础设施，重新定义了发行人进入资本市场的方式；将日常散户投资者与上市公司无缝连接起来。随着经济从冠状病毒的影响中翻滚，随着企业资本重组和经济复苏进程的开始，散户投资者在谈判桌上获得一席之地是势在必行的。“
note_en:
PrimaryBid, which allows retail investors (that is,  ordinary and not professional investors) the ability to invest in new shares issued by public companies, has raised $50 million in new funding of its own. The funding comes on the heels of the startup working alongside larger investment banks to get retail investors in on 41 capital raising efforts for UK publicly-listed companies and trusts since April 2020.
 “The COVID-19 pandemic demonstrated the effectiveness of the public markets, with companies recapitalising quickly and efficiently,” said Anand Sambasivan, CEO of PrimaryBid, in a statement. “Our technology has allowed thousands of retail investors to participate on equal terms with institutional investors, unlocking a large and important source of liquidity and long-term share ownership for corporate issuers. The response from Boards and their advisers to our solution has been excellent: they recognise our digital solution for retail inclusion brings together both good governance and best execution.”
  PrimaryBid plans to use the funds to hire more talent, invest in its tech platform, build out more partnerships and expand internationally.
 Unlike the investors on its platform, this Series B is coming from a list of big-name strategic players and VCs. They include the London Stock Exchange Group, Draper Esprit, OMERS Ventures, Fidelity International Strategic Ventures and ABN AMRO Ventures, as well as previous backers Pentech and Outward Ventures.
 The LSE Group might provide a clue to which geographies might be future targets for PrimaryBid: the Borsa Italiana exchange in Italy, as well as the Turquoise pan-European equities market are also part of the group’s footprint.
 “This investment builds on our collaboration with PrimaryBid and is part of London Stock Exchange Group’s commitment to broadening retail investor access to public equity markets,” said Charlie Walker, Head of Equity and Fixed Income, Primary Markets at London Stock Exchange plc, in a statement. “Through PrimaryBid’s innovative offering, retail investors have been able to access capital raisings on the same terms as institutional investors, supporting the U.K.’s public companies by providing additional capital and liquidity. PrimaryBid has become an important part of the U.K.’s capital raising ecosystem and we look forward to working with them to further enhance retail investor access to capital markets within the U.K. and globally.”
 The startup is not disclosing its valuation with this round, which follows a Series A in September 2019 of  $8.6 million. This latest Series B has been the subject of rumors since  this summer (and most recently a  report last night that the round had finally closed),
 PrimaryBid’s growth comes at a time when a number of startups have been building investment services targeting niche opportunities, and services for those who are underserved.  Rally last month demonstrated that there is definitely money and opportunity in providing a way to invest in (not buy) collectibles;  Yieldstreet has built a platform to introduce investors to new investment classes too like shipping; and companies like  Stash, Revolut and Robinhood are also bringing trading and investing to a new class of consumers.
 That doesn’t mean that new entrants focusing on smaller investors and niche opportunities in the investment market are without their own challenges. Revolut has faced  controversies around the conduct of executives (however, these appear to have been resolved: it’s still raising  hundreds of millions of dollars). YieldStreet  recently sued (and won) a case against a ship recycling company, but at the same time it appears to be  under investigation for some of its practices. And Robinhood  indefinitely postponed its plans to launch in the UK after putting its expansion plans on hold earlier this year.
 PrimaryBid’s recent growth has come on the back of a choppy year in the public markets and the world of investment.
 Just as Covid-19 disrupted other aspects of our life, the early months of the pandemic saw a major freeze descend on the world of trading. With many unsure of how future months might play out in terms of local and global economics, the IPO market all but dried up and trading slowed down.
 Then, things began to thaw, with activity picking up gradually and effectively under new terms: for now, everything is remote. And what’s more, the new playing field means a new opportunity for new players.
 This is where PrimaryBid has been stepping in. The startup has built a platform that makes it easier for retail investors to participate in new share issues, and it has been around since 2016, but it has found its groove at a time when companies raising money might be looking to cast their nets a bit wider than usual.
 The startup led a  big campaign in April to highlight the role that retail investors can play in helping getting the stock market back to active levels. And the companies that have provided access to their new share issuances since the start of the pandemic have included the Compass Group, Ocado, Taylor Wimpey and Segro.
 Retail investors are, in essence, a long-tail play. While individually they will invest considerably less than high net-worth individuals or institutional investors, collectively they account for a substantial amount of activity. The latest figures from the UK’s office of national statistics, from 2018, estimate that retail investors account for some 13.5% of the UK’s share capital, although within the FTSE 250 that is closer to 20% and in some AIM companies it can be as high as 30% or more, according to PrimaryBid.
 There are a number of other platforms for ordinary people to buy and trade shares, but what is different with PrimaryBid is its focus on new share issuances, not sells and trades in existing shares. In theory, a company could also allocate shares to be sold on via PrimaryBid for IPOs, but, as a spokesperson described it, “The real innovation is getting retail involved in ‘accelerated’ follow-on raises (which are around five times the size of IPOs in equity issuance terms), and which have never been open to retail (whereas some IPOs have historically).”
  Vinoth Jayakumar, Partner at Draper Espirit, said: “Our investment in PrimaryBid aligns with part of our wider investment thesis to democratise retail investors access to public markets as well as modernise market infrastructure software. For us, both our companies are anticipating the direction of travel of the future of finance.”
 OMERS Ventures, the investment arm of the prolific pension fund out of Canada, said it’s part of the groups focus on fintech. “As fintech specialists it’s been impossible to ignore the rise of PrimaryBid in 2020 and its success championing retail investors in the capital markets,” said Tara Reeves, Partner at OMERS Ventures, in a statement. “PrimaryBid’s technology sits at the intersection of powerful trends in financial services – regulation, digitalisation and democratisation – and OMERS Ventures is delighted to support the team’s mission to put individual investors on equal terms with institutions. PrimaryBid is now well integrated at the highest levels of the U.K.’s capital raising ecosystem, and we look forward to helping the team realize their ambitions internationally.”
 “We are excited to be partnering with PrimaryBid to enhance fairness, inclusivity, and transparency in capital markets,” said Michael Sim, Vice President, Fidelity International Strategic Ventures, in a statement. “Anand and the team have built unique technology infrastructure that is redefining the way issuers access capital markets; seamlessly connecting everyday retail investors with public companies. As the economy roils from the impact of coronavirus, it is imperative retail investors get a seat at the table as companies recapitalise and the process of economic recovery begins.”
title: 英国PrimaryBid筹集5000万美元，其零售投资平台新冠肺炎活动激增
title_en: UK’s PrimaryBid raises $50M as its retail investing platform sees a Covid-19 surge of activity

note:
Facebook不是为了与Stadia或XCloud竞争而打造游戏机游戏竞争对手，而是将重点完全放在手机游戏上。为什么您的设备已经能够在本地运行云流媒体移动游戏？Facebook的目标是让用户更快地进入游戏，减少用户看到游戏广告和实际玩游戏之间的摩擦。用户可以在不下载任何内容的情况下快速点击标题，如果他们最终选择从移动应用商店下载标题，他们将能够从他们停止的地方继续下载。
Facebook的服务将在桌面网站和Android上推出，但不会在iOS上推出，因为Facebook在苹果应用商店的条款和条件中概述了可用性限制。
虽然苹果在2020年因从应用商店下载的应用的收入中提成多少而遭到了Spotify、叮当和堡垒之夜等主要应用开发商的猛烈批评，但目标是建立云游戏平台的公司的困境则更加微妙，而且与这些平台从根本上被允许在苹果设备上运行的方式有关。
苹果最初在为谷歌(Google)和微软(Microsoft)的云游戏应用提供前进道路方面进展缓慢，此前这两款应用在App Store上是非法的。这家iPhone制造商最近更新了政策，允许这些应用程序存在，但容量比平台制造商希望的更复杂，迫使他们首先将用户送到App Store，然后才能在他们的平台上云流媒体游戏。
对于下载冗长的单人游戏机史诗游戏的用户来说，短暂的停顿会带来不便，但长期担任Facebook游戏高管的杰森·鲁宾(Jason Rubin)表示，这些规定对于Facebook平台设想的立即开始玩手机游戏而不需要下载任何东西的方式来说是行不通的。
鲁宾告诉TechCrunch：“这是一系列的障碍，共同造成了糟糕的消费者体验。”
苹果告诉TechCrunch，他们继续与Facebook接触，将其游戏努力纳入其指导方针之下，各平台可以通过将每款游戏提交给App Store进行审查或在Safari上运营服务来访问iOS。
至于将新平台构建到移动网络上，鲁宾表示，如果不能像目前的规则所禁止的那样，将iOS应用程序的用户引导到基于浏览器的体验，Facebook并不认为推动其数十亿用户主要通过浏览器访问该服务是合理的选择。在一次Zoom Call中，鲁宾演示了这款游戏如何在iOS上操作，用户在应用程序内点击广告，然后被重定向至移动Safari中的游戏体验。
“但如果我点击它，我就不能上网了。鲁宾告诉我们，苹果公司说，‘不，不，你不能这么做。’“苹果可能会说这是一个免费和开放的网络，但你实际上能在这个网络上建立什么是由他们决定在核心功能中投入什么来决定的。”
鲁宾在游戏开发工作室淘气狗(Naughty Dog)于1994年与人共同创立，2001年被索尼收购。自2014年宣布收购Oculus几个月后加入Facebook以来，鲁宾一直在Facebook工作。鲁宾之前的任务是管理虚拟现实耳机的游戏生态系统，今年他被任命为公司的Play副总裁，负责公司核心应用系列的游戏计划。
鲁宾非常熟悉游戏开发商/平台之间的小冲突，他很快就分辨出了脸书与苹果之间的过节，以及像Epic Games这样今年夏天起诉苹果的人的抱怨。
“我确实想确认这样一个事实，即我们(在Android上)给了谷歌30%的佣金。苹果的问题不是钱的问题，“鲁宾告诉TechCrunch。“我们可以讨论谷歌拿走这30%是否公平。但我们现在愿意给苹果30%，如果他们能让消费者有机会做我们在这里提供的服务。“。
值得注意的是，Facebook还从这些游戏的交易中抽取了30%的分成，尽管Facebook的高管团队过去也曾对苹果高昂的营收费用进行过抨击，最近批评苹果的App Store模式在大流行期间损害了小企业的利益。这一事件最终导致苹果公司宣布，对于举办在线活动的小企业的门票销售，它将在今年年底之前暂缓提成。
苹果在允许主要游戏平台独立向消费者提供游戏的道路上保持沉默，突显出App Store收入的很大一部分可能会因消费者转向这些云平台而消失。据CNBC估计，苹果去年从App Store中赚取了约500亿美元，而游戏长期以来一直是他们最赚钱的垂直领域。
尽管Facebook将此描述为与一个主要平台为玩家的利益而进行的一场艰苦的战斗，但这很难说是两个失败者之间的战斗。Facebook去年获得了近700亿美元的广告收入，改善他们为手机游戏工作室提供的服务可能是朝着增加这一数字迈出的有意义的一步，而苹果应用商店的规则威胁到了这一点。
就目前而言，Facebook对此次发布保持相当保守的态度。鲁宾说，在发布时只有5-10款游戏可供选择。Facebook本周将在美国几个州推出这项免费的新服务，包括加利福尼亚州、德克萨斯州、马萨诸塞州、纽约州、新泽西州、康涅狄格州、罗德岛州、特拉华州、宾夕法尼亚州、马里兰州、华盛顿特区、弗吉尼亚州和西弗吉尼亚州。地理推广的大杂烩性质归因于云游戏的技术限制-人们必须靠近服务已经推出的数据中心，才能获得可用的体验。他们说，Facebook的目标是在未来几个月扩大到美国其他地区。
note_en:
Facebook is not building a console gaming competitor to compete with Stadia or  xCloud,   instead the focus is wholly on mobile games. Why cloud stream mobile games that your device is already capable of running locally? Facebook is aiming to get users into games more quickly and put less friction between a user seeing an advertisement for a game and actually playing it themselves. Users can quickly tap into the title without downloading anything and if they eventually opt to download the title from a mobile app store, they’ll be able to pick up where they left off.
 Facebook’s service will launch on the desktop web and  Android,   but not iOS due to what Facebook frames as usability restrictions outlined in Apple’s App Store terms and conditions.
   While Apple has suffered an onslaught of criticism in 2020 from developers of major apps like  Spotify,   Tinder and Fortnite for how much money they take as a cut from revenues of apps downloaded from the App Store, the plights of companies aiming to build cloud gaming platforms have been more nuanced and are tied to how those platforms are fundamentally allowed to operate on Apple devices.
 Apple was initially slow to provide a path forward for cloud gaming apps from  Google   and Microsoft, which had previously been outlawed on the App Store. The iPhone maker recently updated its policies to allow these apps to exist, but in a more convoluted capacity than the platform makers had hoped, forcing them to first send users to the App Store before being able to cloud stream a gaming title on their platform.
  For a user downloading a lengthy single-player console epic, the short pitstop is an inconvenience, but long-time Facebook gaming exec  Jason Rubin   says that the stipulations are a non-starter for what Facebook’s platform envisions, a way to start playing mobile games immediately without downloading anything.
 “It’s a sequence of hurdles that altogether make a bad consumer experience,” Rubin tells TechCrunch.
 Apple tells TechCrunch that they have continued to engage with Facebook on bringing its gaming efforts under its guidelines and that platforms can reach iOS by either submitting each individual game to the App Store for review or operating their service on Safari.
 In terms of building the new platform onto the mobile web, Rubin says that without being able to point users of their iOS app to browser-based experiences, as current rules forbid, Facebook doesn’t see pushing its billions of users to accessing the service primarily from a browser as a reasonable alternative. In a Zoom call, Rubin demoes how this  could operate on iOS, with users tapping an advertisement inside the app and being redirected to a game experience in mobile Safari.
 “But if I click on that, I can’t go to the web. Apple says, ‘No, no, no, no, no, you can’t do that,’ Rubin tells us. “Apple may say that it’s a free and open web, but what you can actually build on that web is dictated by what they decide to put in their core functionality.”
  Rubin, who co-founded the game development studio Naughty Dog in 1994 before it was acquired by  Sony   in 2001, has been at Facebook since he joined Oculus months after its 2014 acquisition was announced. Rubin had previously been tasked with managing the games ecosystem for its virtual reality headsets, this year he was put in charge of the company’s gaming initiatives across their core family of apps as the company’s VP of Play.
 Rubin, well familiar with game developer/platform skirmishes, was quick to distinguish the bone Facebook had to pick with Apple and complaints from those like  Epic Games   which sued Apple this summer.
 “I do want to put a pin in the fact that we’re giving Google 30% [on Android]. The Apple issue is not about money,” Rubin tells TechCrunch. “We can talk about whether or not it’s fair that Google takes that 30%. But we would be willing to give Apple the 30% right now, if they would just let consumers have the opportunity to do what we’re offering here.”
  Facebook is notably also taking a 30% cut of transaction within these games, even as Facebook’s executive team has taken its own shots at Apple’s steep revenue fee in the past, most recently criticizing how Apple’s App Store model was hurting small businesses during the pandemic. This saga  eventually led to Apple announcing that it would withhold its cut through the end of the year for ticket sales of small businesses hosting online events.
 Apple’s reticence to allow major gaming platforms a path towards independently serving up games to consumers underscores the significant portion of App Store revenues that could be eliminated by a consumer shift towards these cloud platforms. Apple earned around $50 billion from the App Store last year, CNBC  estimates, and gaming has long been their most profitable vertical.
 Though Facebook is framing this as an uphill battle against a major platform for the good of the gamer, this is hardly a battle between two underdogs. Facebook pulled in nearly $70 billion in ad revenues last year and improving their offerings for mobile game studios could be a meaningful step towards increasing that number, something Apple’s App Store rules threaten.
 For the time being, Facebook is keeping this launch pretty conservative. There are just 5-10 titles that are going to be available at launch, Rubin says. Facebook is rolling out access to the new service, which is free, this week across a handful of states in America, including California, Texas, Massachusetts, New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, Rhode Island,  Delaware,   Pennsylvania, Maryland, Washington, D.C., Virginia and West Virginia. The hodge-podge nature of the geographic rollout is owed to the technical limitations of cloud-gaming– people have to be close to data centers where the service has rolled out in order to have a usable experience. Facebook is aiming to scale to the rest of the U.S. in the coming months, they say.
title: Facebook涉足云游戏-与苹果又一次恩怨
title_en: Facebook steps into cloud gaming — and another feud with Apple

note:
水质和物流监测软件Ketos已经从一群投资者那里筹集了1500万美元，以利用人们对更好的水管理工具和技术日益增长的需求。
水监测还可以向政府官员提供关于人口中爆发疾病的可能性和其他与健康有关的问题的相关信息。
最近，污水监测已经被用来检测导致新冠肺炎的病毒的爆发。
对水的重新关注是银行业巨头花旗(Citi)的一个投资部门加入主要投资者Motley Fool Ventures和Lighted Funds Group作为新投资者进入Ketos的原因之一。他们加入了Ajax Strategy、Better Ventures、百老汇天使(Broadway Angels)、梅谷风险投资公司(Plum Valley Ventures)和Reink Impact等现有支持者的行列。
该公司表示，将利用这笔资金为其综合硬件和软件服务开发新的功能，该服务提供有关水质以及分配和处置用水的水管是否存在潜在损坏的信息。
该公司创始人兼首席执行官米娜·桑卡兰(Meena Sankaran)说，“创建一个最大的水质洞察力集中化数据湖-拥有关于重金属毒素的信息，再加上基于位置的地图和潜在的污染源-机器学习和人工智能可以实现的潜力是无限的，”该公司创始人兼首席执行官米娜·桑卡兰(Meena Sankaran)说。
另一个卖点是该公司使用机器学习来预测供水系统可能出现问题的地方-避免了对基础设施进行更昂贵的投资。
Motley Fool Ventures董事总经理奥伦·道格拉斯(Ollen DouGlass)在一份声明中表示：“Ketos自主(远程控制)捕获的数据正在真正颠覆水情报行业，并向客户提供用于预测水管理问题的数据，在世界与COVID作斗争的过程中，这甚至是人们最关心的问题，”Motley Fool Ventures董事总经理奥伦·道格拉斯(Ollen DouGlass)在一份声明中表示。通过0美元的资本基础设施投资，首次可以使用预测性建模和亟需的任务关键型洞察力来建设、采取行动并做出有关供水网络的明智决策。“
note_en:
Water quality and logistics monitoring software  Ketos has raised $15 million from a group of investors to take advantage of the growing demand for better water management tools and technologies.
  Water monitoring can also provide relevant information to public officials about the potential for disease outbreaks and other health related issues in a population.
  Recently, monitoring wastewater streams have been used to detect outbreaks of the virus that causes COVID-19.
 The renewed attention on water is one reason why an investment arm of the banking giant  Citi   joined lead investor Motley Fool Ventures and Illuminated Funds Group to come as new investors into Ketos. They joined existing backers like Ajax Strategies, Better Ventures, Broadway Angels, Plum Valley Ventures, and Rethink Impact.
  The company said it would use the funding to develop new capabilities for its combined hardware and software service that provides information into water quality and the existence of potential damage to water pipes for distribution and disposal of water.
 “Creating one of the largest centralized data lakes of water quality insights — with information on heavy-metal toxins, coupled with location-based mapping and potential contamination sources — the potential for what machine learning and artificial intelligence can achieve is limitless,” said Meena Sankaran, the company’s founder and chief executive.
 One other selling point is the company’s use of machine learning to predict where problems with water systems might arise — avoiding the need for more costly investments into infrastructure.
 “KETOS is truly disrupting the water intelligence industry with the data it captures autonomously (remotely controlled) and makes available to its customers for forecasting water management issues, which is even more top of mind as the world battles COVID,” said Ollen Douglass, Managing Director of Motley Fool Ventures, in a statement. “For the first time, it is possible to use predictive modeling and much needed mission-critical insights with $0 capital infrastructure investments, to build, take action and make informed decisions about a water network.”
title: 水质和分配监测软件Ketos筹集1800万美元
title_en: Water quality and distribution monitoring software Ketos raises $18 million

note:
这家墨尔本初创公司表示，已有800万用户-无论是赛琳娜·戈麦斯(Selena Gomez)和杜瓦·利帕(Dua Lipa)等名人，还是HBO和红牛(Red Bull)等品牌-在该平台上创建了个人资料，这些个人资料在9月份收到了超过10亿的访问量。
首席执行官亚历克斯·扎卡里亚(Alex Zaccaria)告诉我：“我们刚开始的时候，这个类别还不存在。”“我们创建了这个类别。”
扎卡里亚说，他和他的联合创始人安东尼·扎卡里亚(Anthony Zaccaria)和尼克·汉弗莱斯(Nick Humphreys)创建Linktree是为了解决他们在数字营销机构BOUSTER面临的问题。Instagram不允许用户在帖子中包含链接--你得到的只是个人资料中的一个链接，当有人想要推广什么东西时，它会不断提示“简历中的链接”。
与此同时，Borster的大部分客户来自音乐和娱乐行业，在这两个领域，单一的链接无法支持扎卡里亚所说的“相当分散”的商业模式。毕竟，艺术家可能想把歌迷引向他们最新的流媒体专辑、即将到来的演唱会日期、商品的在线商店等等。从理论上讲，网站可以做到这一点，但在移动设备上，它们可能会很笨重或速度很慢，用户可能会在最终到达想要的页面之前就放弃了。
因此，Linktree用户不必经常交换Instagram和其他社交媒体个人资料中的链接，而是包括一个指向其Linktree个人资料的常青树链接，他们可以根据需要更新该链接。例如，赛琳娜·戈麦斯(Selena Gomez)不仅链接到她最新的歌曲和视频，还链接到她的稀有美容化妆品品牌、她的官方商店和关于她的非营利性工作的文章。
扎卡里亚说，在2016年推出这款产品后，团队很快发现“有更多的人有同样的问题”，导致他们在两年前完全分离了Linktree和Boster。从那时起，该公司就没有筹集过任何外部资金-直到现在，由Insight Partners和AirTree Ventures牵头的1070万美元的首轮融资。
“我们可以选择继续可持续增长，但我们想火上浇油，”扎卡里亚说。
事实上，Linktree今年已经从10名员工增加到50名员工。虽然该公司一开始是为Instagram用户解决问题，但扎卡里亚将其描述为一个更广泛的平台，可以“统一你的整个数字生态系统”，“让数字存在民主化”。他说，虽然一些客户继续维护“一个巨大的、让人身临其境的品牌网站”，但对另一些客户来说，Linktree正在完全取代独立网站的想法。
Zaccaria补充说，Instagram只代表Linktree目前的一小部分流量，而现在近25%的流量来自直接访问者。
黑人生命也是Linktree最近增长的一大部分，想要支持这项运动的活动家和其他用户使用他们的个人资料将访问者引导到他们可以捐赠、了解更多和参与的网站。事实上，Linktree甚至在夏天推出了一个黑人生命也是横幅，任何人都可以添加到他们的个人资料中。
Linktree是免费使用的，但你必须每月支付6美元购买专业功能，如视频链接、链接缩略图和社交媒体图标。
扎卡里亚说，新的资金将允许这家初创公司增加更多的“功能和分析”。他特别渴望扩大数据科学和分析团队，尽管他强调Linktree不会以任何方式收集个人身份信息或将访问者数据货币化-他只是想向Linktree用户提供更多数据。
随着互联网变得越来越分散，品牌、出版商和有影响力的人需要一个解决方案来简化他们的内容共享，并将他们的社交媒体追随者连接到他们的整个在线生态系统，最终提高品牌知名度和收入。Linktree已经成功地创建了这一新的“微型网站”类别，使公司能够通过一个单一的互动中心将下一代互联网经济货币化。Linktree在过去几个月中获得的令人印象深刻的吸引力和不断增长的客户数量证明了它已被证实的市场适应性，在Linktree团队过渡到规模增长阶段的过程中，我们非常高兴能与他们合作。
note_en:
The Melbourne startup says that 8 million users — whether they’re celebrities like Selena Gomez and Dua Lipa or brands like HBO and Red Bull — have created profiles on the platform, with those profiles receiving more than 1 billion visitors in September.
   “This category didn’t exist when we started,” CEO Alex Zaccaria told me. “We  created this category.”
 Zaccaria said that he and his co-founders Anthony Zaccaria and Nick Humphreys created Linktree to solve a problem they were facing at their digital marketing agency  Bolster. Instagram doesn’t allow users to include links in posts — all you get is a single link in your profile, prompting the constant “link in bio” reminder when someone wants to promote something.
 Meanwhile, most of Bolster’s clients come from music and entertainment, where a single link can’t support what Zaccaria said is a “quite fragmented” business model. After all, an artist might want to point fans to their latest streaming album, upcoming concert dates, an online store for merchandise and more. A website could do the job in theory, but they can be clunky or slow on mobile, with users probably giving up before they finally reach the desired page.
  So instead of constantly swapping out links in Instagram and other social media profiles, a Linktree user includes one evergreen link to their Linktree profile, which they can update as necessary.  Selena Gomez, for example, links to her latest songs and videos, but also her Rare Beauty cosmetics brand, her official store and articles about her nonprofit work.
 Zaccaria said that after launching the product in 2016, the team quickly discovered that “a lot more people had the same problem,” leading them to fully separate Linktree and Bolster two years ago. Since then, the company hasn’t raised any outside funding — until now, with a $10.7 million Series A led by  Insight Partners   and AirTree Ventures.
 “We had the option to just continue to grow sustainably, but we wanted to pour some fuel on the fire,” Zaccaria said.
 In fact, Linktree has already grown from 10 to 50 employees this year. And while the company started out by solving a problem for Instagram users, Zaccaria described it as evolving into a much broader platform that can “unify your entire digital ecosystem” and “democratize digital presence.” He said that while some customers continue to maintain “a giant, brand-immersive website,” for others, Linktree is completely replacing the idea of a standalone website.
 Zaccaria added that Instagram only represents a small amount of Linktree’s current traffic, while nearly 25% of that traffic now comes from direct visitors.
  Black Lives Matter has also been a big part of Linktree’s recent growth, with activists and other users who want to support the movement using their profiles to point visitors to websites where they can donate, learn more and get involved. In fact, Linktree even  introduced a Black Lives Matter banner over the summer that anyone could add to their profile.
 Linktree is free to use, but you have to pay $6 a month for Pro features like video links, link thumbnails and social media icons.
 Zaccaria said that the new funding will allow the startup to add more “functionality and analytics.” He’s particularly eager to grow the data science and analytics team, though he emphasized that Linktree does not collect personally identifiable information or monetize visitor data in any way — he just wants to provide more data to Linktree users.
  As the internet becomes increasingly fragmented, brands, publishers, and influencers need a solution to streamline their content sharing and connect their social media followers to their entire online ecosystem, ultimately increasing brand awareness and revenue. Linktree has successfully created this new “microsite” category enabling companies to monetize the next generation of the internet economy via a single interactive hub. The impressive traction and growing number of customers Linktree has gained over the last few months demonstrates its proven market fit, and we could not be more excited to work with the Linktree team as they transition to the ScaleUp phase of growth.
title: Linktree为其轻量级、以链接为中心的用户配置文件筹集了1070万美元
title_en: Linktree raises $10.7M for its lightweight, link-centric user profiles

note:
Dropbox的现代基础设施故事可以追溯到2017年，当时它决定将大部分业务从托管在AWS上转移到建设自己的基础设施上。在接下来的几年里，随着他们完成这一过渡过程，他们正在寻找新的存储技术想法，以帮助降低运行自己的海量存储系统的成本。
正如总工程师詹姆斯·考林(James Cowling)去年告诉TechCrunch的那样，出现的想法之一是使用SMR：
出现了SMR，它具有高存储密度和较低的价位。转移到SMR使Dropbox能够以更少的成本做更多的事情，提高效率和降低总体成本-对于试图独立完成这一任务的公司来说，这是必不可少的一步。考林说：“这显然需要专业知识，但在降低软件和硬件之间的界限的同时，还能在成本和存储效率方面带来很多效率，这也是令人兴奋的。”
事实证明，Dropbox工程副总裁安德鲁·方(Andrew Fong)表示，该公司与西部数据合作多年，新的SMR技术是合作伙伴关系中的最新一步。
西部数据表示，这些驱动器通过增加存储密度和降低电力需求来节省成本。该公司在一份声明中表示：“当考虑到艾字节规模的需求，以及数据中心的相关资本和运营成本时，较低的每TB成本、较高的密度、较低的功耗和较高的可靠性方面的长期价值可以帮助受益于底线，”该公司在一份声明中表示。
时间会证明这些磁盘是否能如期交付，但对于像Dropbox这样的大容量用户来说，它们肯定显示出很大的潜力。
note_en:
Dropbox’s   modern infrastructure story goes back to 2017 when it decided to shift most of its business  from being hosted on AWS to building their own infrastructure. As they moved through the process of making that transition in the following years, they were looking for new storage technology ideas to help drive down the cost of running their own massive storage system.
 As principal engineer James Cowling  told TechCrunch last year, one of the ideas that emerged was using SMR:
  What emerged was SMR, which has high storage density and a lower price point. Moving to SMR gave Dropbox the ability to do more with less, increasing efficiency and lowering overall costs — an essential step for a company trying to do this on its own. “It required expertise obviously, but it was also exciting to bring a lot of efficiencies in terms of cost and storage efficiency, while pulling down boundaries between software and hardware,” Cowling said.
  As it turns out, Dropbox VP of engineering Andrew Fong says that the company has been working with  Western Digital   for a number of years and the new SMR technology is the latest step in that partnership.
 Western Digital says that these drives deliver this cost savings through increased storage density and lower power requirements. “When considering exabyte-scale needs, and associated capital and operating cost of the data center, the long-term value in terms of lower cost-per-TB, higher density, low power and high reliability can help benefit the bottom line,” the company said in a statement.
 Time will tell if these disks deliver as promised, but they certainly show a lot of potential for a high volume user like Dropbox.
title: Dropbox开始转向高效率西部数据瓦片磁记录磁盘
title_en: Dropbox begins shift to high efficiency Western Digital Shingled Magnetic Recording disks

note_en:
Hello and welcome back to  Equity, TechCrunch’s venture capital-focused podcast where we unpack the numbers behind the headlines.
 This is Equity Monday, our weekly kickoff that tracks the latest big news, chats about the coming week, digs into some recent funding rounds and mulls over a larger theme or narrative from the private markets. You can follow the show on Twitter  here and myself  here — and don’t forget to check out  last Friday’s episode that includes some high-quality Quibi jokes, if I recall correctly.
 This was a busy morning, with lots to talk about it. Here’s what we got into:
 Headlines this morning that caught our eye included falling global stock markets in the wake of rising COVID-19 cases; SAP torched tens of billions of market cap by  missing earnings expectations and cutting its forecast; and  Ant Group will raise a bajillion dollars in its impending IPO.
 In unicorn news, Databricks is  prepping an IPO that  we already know a lot about. And  Airbnb’s shares are splitting ahead of its own IPO, a  deal that we are expecting any day now.
 The  PrimaryBid round was super cool, with TechCrunch covering the $50 million deal. I spoke with the company’s CEO last week about the deal, sharing some of those notes on the show. And  VSPN raised nearly $100 million in an esports round that was more than cool.
 On the SAP front, here’s the question: Is the earnings miss and forecast miss a one-off, or something that a number of tech companies are going to suffer from? If the former, it’s not  that big of a deal. If the latter, SAP could be the harbinger of a reset in tech valuations.
  Equity drops every Monday at 7:00 a.m. PT and Thursday afternoon as fast as we can get it out, so subscribe to us on   Apple Podcasts ,   Overcast ,   Spotify  and all the casts.

作为致力于支持风险投资和技术领域性别平等的组织All Raise的创始人之一，Times Up的董事，以及总部位于洛杉矶的扩张女子足球队天使城(Angel City)的联合创始人，诺特曼一直在她的职业和个人生活中倡导平等。
现在，随着诺特曼(和她的公司)晋升为前置风险投资公司的联席管理合伙人，她又迈出了一大步，推动该行业的女性向前迈进了一步。她的晋升很可能是大型风险投资公司首次提拔女性担任联席管理合伙人。
从历史上看，风险投资行业的女性必须推出自己的基金，才能在该行业担任领导角色。诺顿的晋升与此背道而驰-很可能是因为认识到时代已经改变，公司需要适应这样一个世界，在这个世界里，企业家经常选择从与他们的价值观和战略愿景保持一致的公司获得资本。
对于Upfront的最新联席管理合伙人来说，在一家管理着约20亿美元资产的基金中，领导之路始于深夜，当时他是马萨诸塞州Battery Partners的合伙人。
诺特曼回忆说：“我23岁时一直在办公室看”麻省理工学院“和”红鲱鱼“，一直读到晚上10点。”“没有太多的数据可以用来搜集、提取和运行算法。”
在Battery，诺特曼专注于企业软件，之后转到企业界，担任互动公司(Interactive Corp.)的高管。这位洛杉矶人自2014年以来一直是Upfront的合伙人，在那里她投资了Parachant Home(她的第一笔前期投资)等零售公司，最近又投资了OpenRaven和Fleetsmith等企业软件公司(这是她作为前期合作伙伴的第一次重大退出，被苹果收购)。
诺特曼说：“我的个人投资组合大约有30%是消费者，70%是深度科技企业。”在我职业生涯的早期，我在Battery…做过所有的企业和基础设施工作。因为那就是全部。在1999年的…中，没有消费者可以投资。Webvan和Pets.com都不是好主意。
在前期，诺特曼将继续与管理合伙人马克·苏斯特一起工作，领导公司的基金投资活动。
苏斯特在一份声明中说：“如果每个告诉我他们有多爱卡拉的创始人或风险投资人都有一美元，我就会有10倍的基金。”卡拉是一位天生的领导者，她作为All Raise的创始成员、Times Up的董事会成员以及将女子职业足球队天使城足球俱乐部带到洛杉矶的工作就证明了这一点。我们在内部给予她更多的领导力，就是对她在这里已经扮演的角色的认可。“。
诺特曼在洛杉矶风投社区受到普遍尊重，几位投资者表示，此举对该公司来说是一个极好的战略选择。据我所知，这也是一个历史性的事件。
虽然几十年来一直有才华横溢、资历出众的女性在风险投资领域工作，但几乎没有人能够通过从内部晋升来担任领导职位。詹妮弗·方斯塔特(Jennifer Fonstadt)和特蕾西亚·高(Tthersia Gouw)分别离开了DFJ和Accel，成立了自己的基金。特雷·瓦萨洛(Trae Vassallo)、艾琳·李(Aileen Lee)和贝丝·塞登伯格(Beth Sedenberg。
诺特曼表示，合作伙伴名单上包括科比·富勒(Kobie Fuller)、格雷格·贝蒂内利(Greg Bettinelli)、阿迪蒂·马里瓦尔(Aditi Maliwal)和迈克尔·卡尼(Michael Carney)，不会有任何其他变化。
该公司的战略也不会有任何改变，即该公司将大约一半的资本投资于大洛杉矶地区，其余部分分散在美国、欧洲和以色列的交易中。
Up Front的成功伴随着洛杉矶科技生态系统的更广泛扩张，来自“指环”(Ring)、TestFlight和Maker Studios等家乡英雄的大量退出，以及对伯德(Bird)、阿皮尔科学(Apeel Sciences)和山羊(Good)等南加州后起之秀的投资。
对于诺特曼来说，他目睹了这个行业的许多变化，最大的变化是随着公司的发展，对专业化的需求。诺特曼说：“过去，你可以干脆跑一趟就行了。”“在如何打造品牌方面，你必须真正自律。”
除此之外，投资者在选择投资时需要更具战略性，因为持有期更长，公司正在处理的市场比20年前要大得多。
“我几乎是我加入的每一家公司的第一位董事会成员。我可能会在董事会呆上10到15年，“诺特曼说。“如今，你需要更快地做出决策和打造品牌，但承诺的时间要长得多。”
note_en:
As one of the founders of the All Raise, the organization dedicated to supporting gender equity in venture capital and technology, a director for Times Up and as the co-founder of the Los Angeles-based expansion women’s soccer team, Angel City, Nortman has been a voice for equality in her professional and personal life.
 Now with her promotion to co-managing partner at Upfront Ventures, Nortman (and her firm) are taking another big step to advance women in the industry. Her promotion represents what is likely the first time that a large venture capital firm has promoted a woman to the position of co-managing partner.
  Historically women in the venture capital industry have had to launch their own funds to assume a leadership role in the industry. Norton’s promotion flies in the face of that — likely as a recognition that times have changed and firms need to adapt to a world where entrepreneurs are often choosing to take capital from firms that align with their values as much as their strategic vision.
 For Upfront’s newest co-managing partner, the path to leadership at a fund with roughly $2 billion in assets under management began with late nights as an associate with Battery Partners in Massachusetts.
 “I was the 23 year-old at the office reading MassSciTech and Red Herring until 10 at night,” Nortman recalled. “There wasn’t a ton of data to scrape and pull and run algorithms against.”
 At Battery, Nortman focused on enterprise software before moving over to the corporate world as an executive at Interactive Corp. The Los Angeles native has been a partner at Upfront since 2014 where she’s invested in retail companies like Parachute Home (her first Upfront investment) and more recently in enterprise software companies like OpenRaven and Fleetsmith (which was acquired by Apple in her first big exit as an Upfront partner).
 “My personal portfolio is about 30% consumer and 70% deeptech enterprise,” said Nortman. “I did all enterprise and infrastructure early in my career at Battery … because that’s all there was. There was no consumer to invest in 1999… Webvan and Pets.com were not good ideas.”
 At Upfront Nortman will continue to work alongside managing partner, Mark Suster, leading the firm’s fund investment activities.
 “If I had a dollar for every founder or VC who told me  how much they loved Kara I’d have a 10x fund,” Suster said in a statement. “Kara is a natural leader as evidenced by her  role as a founding member of All Raise, a board member of TIMES UP and in bringing the  women’s professional soccer team, Angel City Football Club, to Los Angeles. Our giving her  more leadership inside of Upfront is just a recognition of the role she already plays here.”
 Nortman is universally respected in the Los Angeles venture community with several investors saying that the move was an excellent strategic choice for the firm. It’s also (as far as I can tell) a historic one.
 While there have been amazingly talented and qualified women working in venture capital for decades, few if any were able to assume leadership positions by being promoted from within. Jennifer Fonstadt and Theresia Gouw left DFJ and Accel (respectively) to launch their own fund. And Trae Vassallo, Aileen Lee and Beth Seidenberg all spent time at Kleiner Perkins before striking out on their own to launch independent funds.
 Nortman said there won’t be any other changes to the partnership whose roster of partners includes Kobie Fuller, Greg Bettinelli, Aditi Maliwal and Michael Carney.
 Nor will there be any changes to the strategy which has seen the firm invest roughly half of its capital in the greater Los Angeles region with the rest spread across deals in the U.S., Europe and Israel.
 Upfront’s success has tracked the broader expansion of the tech ecosystem in Los Angeles with big exits coming from hometown heroes like Ring, TestFlight and Maker Studios along with investments in rising Southern California stars like Bird, Apeel Sciences, and GOAT.
 For Nortman, who has seen a lot of changes in the industry, the biggest is the need for specialization as firm’s grow. “It used to be that you could just hustle,” Nortman said. “You have to be really disciplined about how you build brands.”
 Beyond that, there’s a need for investors to be more strategic about picking their investments, because hold periods are longer and the markets companies are tackling are exponentially larger than 20 years ago.
 “I’m the first board member of almost every company I join. And I may be on the board for ten to 15 years,” Nortman said. “These days you need to be faster to make decisions and build brand but the commitment level is much much longer.”
title: 现在，作为联席管理合伙人，Upfront的卡拉·诺特曼(Kara Nortman)是首批晋升为风投公司领导层的女性之一
title_en: Now a co-managing partner, Upfront’s Kara Nortman is one of the first women promoted to a leadership role in VC

总部设在伦敦的金融科技今天宣布提高大约。4230万欧元的B系列融资，由OMERS、伦敦证券交易所集团(London Stock Exchange Group)和法国德雷珀ESPRIT(Draper ESPRIT)以及富达(Fidelity)以及现有投资者Pentech和Outside VC等共同牵头。
成立于2015年的PrimaryBid将日常投资者与上市公司无缝连接。通过与伦敦证券交易所(London Stock Exchange)达成的一项长期协议，它确保散户投资者与机构投资者在同一时间、以相同的价格进行交易，从而创造了更民主的公开市场准入。到目前为止，它已经参与了伦敦市场上的90只股票发行，包括Compass Group、Ocado、泰勒·温佩比和威廉·希尔(William Hill)。
自2020年4月以来，PrimaryBid已为英国上市公司和投资信托基金完成了逾41笔融资，随着企业对资产负债表进行资本重组并筹集增长资本，PrimaryBid与全球投行合作，拓宽了投资者渠道。散户投资者直接拥有英国市场13.5%的份额，同时额外持有约2967亿欧元(2690亿英镑)的现金ISA，使他们成为任何融资活动的重要支持者(资料来源：ONS，2018年；HMRC，2020年)。
PrimaryBid联合创始人兼首席执行官阿南德·桑巴西文评论说：“在我们向国际扩张并将目光投向北美的过程中，我们很高兴将德雷珀ESPRIT作为投资者和合作伙伴。我们简单、包容的技术具有全球适用性。它使公司发行人能够接触到尽可能广泛的投资者，改善了资本形成，并使公众重新进入公开市场。“。
Draper ESPRIT合伙人Vinoth Jayakumar评论说：“我们对PrimaryBid的投资符合我们更广泛的投资理念的一部分，即让散户投资者进入公开市场的民主化，以及市场基础设施软件的现代化。对我们来说，我们两家公司都在预判金融未来的行进方向。“。
伦敦证券交易所公司股票和固定收益初级市场部主管查理·沃克说：“这项投资建立在我们与PrimaryBid合作的基础上，是伦敦证券交易所集团扩大散户投资者进入公开股票市场的承诺的一部分。通过PrimaryBid的创新产品，散户投资者能够以与机构投资者相同的条件获得融资，通过提供额外的资本和流动性来支持英国的上市公司。PrimaryBid已经成为英国融资生态系统的重要组成部分，我们期待着与他们合作，进一步加强散户投资者进入英国国内和全球资本市场的机会。“。
OMERS Ventures合伙人塔拉·里夫斯(Tara Reeves)表示：“作为金融科技的专家，我们不可能忽视PrimaryBid在2020年的崛起及其在资本市场上倡导散户投资者的成功。PrimaryBid的技术处于金融服务领域强大趋势-监管、数字化和民主化-的交汇点，OMERS Ventures很高兴支持团队的使命，即让个人投资者与机构处于平等地位。”PrimaryBid现在已经很好地融入了英国融资生态系统的最高级别，我们期待着帮助这个团队实现他们在国际上的雄心壮志。“
note_en:
London-based fintech  PrimaryBid has today announced raising approx. €42.3 million Series B funding, co-led by OMERS, London Stock Exchange Group and Draper Esprit, alongside Fidelity, and existing investors Pentech and Outward VC among others.
 Founded in 2015, PrimaryBid seamlessly connects everyday investors with public companies. Through a long-term agreement with the London Stock Exchange, it ensures that retail investors transact at the same time and at the same price as institutional investors, creating a more democratised access to public markets. To date it has been involved in 90 issuances on London markets, including Compass Group, Ocado, Taylor Wimpey and William Hill.
 So far, PrimaryBid has completed over 41 capital raisings for UK listed companies and investment trusts since April 2020, working alongside global investment banks to broaden investor access as companies recapitalised their balance sheets and raised growth capital. Retail investors directly own 13.5% of the UK market, while additionally holding approximately €296.7 billion (£269 billion) in cash ISAs, making them an important constituency in any capital raising activity (sources: ONS, 2018; HMRC, 2020).
 Anand Sambasivan, co-founder and CEO at PrimaryBid, commented:   “We are delighted to count Draper Esprit as both an investor and a partner as we expand internationally, and look toward North America. Our simple, inclusive technology has global applicability. It allows corporate issuers to access the widest possible set of investors, improving capital formation and putting the public back into public markets.”
 Vinoth Jayakumar, Partner at Draper Esprit, commented:   “Our investment in PrimaryBid aligns with part of our wider investment thesis to democratise retail investors’ access to public markets as well as modernise market infrastructure software. For us, both our companies are anticipating the direction  of travel of the future of finance.”
 Charlie Walker, Head of Equity and Fixed Income, Primary Markets at London Stock Exchange plc said: “This investment builds on our collaboration with PrimaryBid and is part of London Stock Exchange Group’s commitment to broadening retail investor access to public equity markets. Through PrimaryBid’s innovative offering, retail investors have been able to access capital raisings on the same terms as institutional investors, supporting the U.K.’s public companies by providing additional capital and liquidity. PrimaryBid has become an important part of the U.K.’s capital raising ecosystem and we look forward to working with them to further enhance retail investor access to capital markets within the U.K. and globally.”
 Tara Reeves, Partner at OMERS Ventures said:  “As fintech specialists it’s been impossible to ignore the rise of PrimaryBid in 2020 and its success championing retail investors in the capital markets.PrimaryBid’s technology sits at the intersection of powerful trends in financial services – regulation, digitalisation and democratisation – and OMERS Ventures is delighted to support the team’s mission to put individual investors on equal terms with institutions. PrimaryBid is now well integrated at the highest levels of the U.K.’s capital raising ecosystem, and we look forward to helping the team realize their ambitions internationally.”
title: 总部位于伦敦的PrimaryBid开发连接散户投资者和上市公司的软件，筹集4230万欧元的B系列资金
title_en: London-based PrimaryBid, which develops software connecting retail investors with public companies, raises €42.3M Series B

苹果、Netflix和其他流媒体服务公司探索了收购即将上映的电影“阿凡达”的可能性，这部电影原定于去年4月上映。这部电影的上映已经多次推迟，丹尼尔·克雷格(Daniel Craig)的车辆被推迟到11月，然后随着冠状病毒病例的数量持续增长被推迟到2021年。
内部人士说，据报道，这部电影背后的制片厂米高梅因延误而损失了3000万至5000万美元。彭博社(Bloomberg)最先报道了这些讨论，这是本周好莱坞的当务之急。其他电影公司，如派拉蒙和索尼，在展览业继续在大流行期间苦苦挣扎的同时，通过向流媒体服务销售“灰狗”(Greyhound)、“降临2美国”(Coming 2 America)和“无怨无悔”等电影，赚取了数千万美元的收入。
“我们对传言不予置评。这部电影是非卖品。这部电影已经推迟到2021年4月上映，以便为影迷保留影院体验。“米高梅的一位发言人告诉”综艺“杂志。
然而，竞争对手制片厂和公司的多名内部人士表示，已经公开探索了出售债券的可能性，并认为米高梅至少对以高额出售其皇冠上的明珠的可能性持开放态度。据说，该制片厂正在寻找一笔大约6亿美元的交易-这个价格被认为对两家大手大脚的流媒体服务来说太高了。内部人士说，如此大规模的出售将由米高梅大股东安克雷奇资本集团(Anclage Capital Group)董事长兼首席执行官凯文·乌尔里希(Kevin Ulrich)独家领导。
目前还不清楚制片人芭芭拉·布罗科利(Barbara Broccoli)和迈克尔·G·威尔逊(Michael G.Wilson)是否会签署这笔交易，他们通过自己的公司意昂(Eon)控制了这部电视剧。拥有“没有时间死去”(No Time To Die)海外发行权的环球影业，在任何可能的出售中都必须做到完整，并偿还制片厂发生的任何费用。值得注意的是，相关各方将探索流媒体销售，因为这部电影是冠状病毒升级为全球大流行之前第一个推迟上映日期的触角-这使得它成为一个早期迹象，表明即使是标志性的间谍和女士们也不会把我们从病毒式事件中拯救出来。
将“没有时间死去”转移到流媒体服务会带来一些后勤挑战。这部电影的制作成本超过2.5亿美元，并安排了几个宣传合作伙伴来帮助支付这些成本-包括路虎(Land Rover)、欧米茄手表(Omega Watch)和喜力(Heineken)。这些公司可能一直在期待这部电影在影院上映，可能不会对仅限流媒体的鞠躬感到兴奋。例如，“即将到来的2个美国”出售给亚马逊的条件是确保其促销合作伙伴麦当劳和皇冠皇家参与计划的改变。
note_en:
Apple, Netflix and other streaming services explored the possibility of acquiring “ ,” the upcoming   movie that was originally slated to debut last April. The film’s release has been postponed multiple times, with the Daniel Craig vehicle moving back to November before being pushed into 2021 as the number of coronavirus cases kept growing.
 MGM, the studio behind the film, reportedly lost between $30 million to $50 million due to the delays, insiders said. Bloomberg first reported the discussions, which have been the topic du jour in Hollywood this week. Other studios, such as Paramount and Sony, have raked in tens of millions by selling movies like “Greyhound,” “Coming 2 America” and “Without Remorse” to streaming services while the exhibition sector continues to struggle during the pandemic.
  “We do not comment on rumors. The film is not for sale. The film’s release has been postponed until April 2021 in order to preserve the theatrical experience for moviegoers,” an MGM spokesperson told  Variety.
 However, multiple insiders at rival studios and companies said that a possible Bond sale was explored overtly, and believe that MGM was at least open to the possibility of unloading their crown jewel for a princely sum. The studio was said to be looking for a deal of roughly $600 million — a price tag that was deemed too rich for two of the free-spending streaming services. A sale of this magnitude would be led exclusively by Kevin Ulrich, the chairman and CEO of MGM’s majority owner Anchorage Capital Group, insiders said.
 It’s unclear if producers Barbara Broccoli and Michael G. Wilson, who exert control of the series through their company Eon, would sign off on the deal. Universal Pictures, which has foreign distribution rights to “No Time to Die,” would have to be made whole in any possible sale and reimbursed for any expenses the studio incurred. That the parties involved would explore a streaming sale is notable, given that the film was the first tentpole to move release dates before coronavirus was upgraded to a global pandemic — making it an early indicator that even the iconic spy and ladies man would not save us from the viral event.
 Moving “No Time to Die” to a streaming service poses some logistical challenges. The film costs more than $250 million to produce and has lined up several promotional partnerships to help defray those costs — including Land Rover, Omega watches and Heineken. Those companies may have been expecting the film to hit theaters and might not be thrilled with a streaming-only bow. “Coming 2 America’s” sale to Amazon, for instance, was contingent on making sure that its promotional partners, McDonald’s and Crown Royal, were on board with the change in plans.
title: 消息来源：Apple TV+，Netflix和其他公司探索购买《没有时间死去》的版权，但对米高梅6亿美元的价格犹豫不决
title_en: Sources: Apple TV+, Netflix, and others explored buying the rights for “No Time to Die”, but balked at MGM's $600M price tag
